sections.paragraph:
ἀπαιτέω 1 aor. pass. ptc. ἀπαιτηθείς Wsd 15:8; fut. 2 sg. pass. ἀπαιτηθήσῃ Is 30:33; inf. ἀπαιτηθήσεσθαι (Just., A I, 17, 4) and ἀπαιτήσεσθαι (Ath. 36, 1) (s. αἰτέω; Trag., Hdt.+)

① to demand someth. back or as due, ask for, demand of a loan (as in ‘payment on demand’=dun) or stolen property (Theophr., Char. 10, 2; Phalaris, Ep. 83, 1; 2; SIG 955, 18; pap, e.g., BGU 183, 8; PYadin 17, 9 al.; Poxy 3058, 13 [II a.d.]; Sir 20:15; Philo, De Jos. 227; Just., D. 125, 2 τὰ ἴδια παρὰ πάντων) τὶ ἀπό τινος Lk 6:30. W. obj. supplied from the context ἀπό τινος Hs 8, 1, 5; cp. D 1:4. Abs. 1:5. Using the image of life as a loan (Cicero, De Rep. 1, 3, 4; cp. Epict. 4, 1, 172; Wsd 15:8; other reff. Horst, Ps.-Phocyl., p. 190) τὴν ψυχὴν ἀπαιτοῦσιν ἀπὸ σοῦ they are demanding your soul fr. you, i.e. your soul will be demanded of you Lk 12:20 (v.l. αἰτοῦσιυ).

② gener. to ask for with a note of urgency, demand, desire (Diod S 16, 56, 3; Dio Chrys. 31, 3; Jos., Ant. 12, 181; PBerl 11662, 26 [I a.d.]=Olsson 34, p. 100: ὁ τόπος ἀπαιτεῖ=the place demands) ὁ καιρὸς ἀπαιτεῖ σε the time demands you, i.e. a person like you IPol 2:3 (Procop. Soph., Ep. 54 καιρὸς γράμματα ἀπαιτῶν=time that demands a letter). 1 Pt 3:15 v.l.—DELG s.v. αἰτέω. M-M. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

inf. inf. = infinitive

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

someth. someth. = something

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

PYadin PYadin = The Documents from the Bar Kokhba Period named in honor of YYadin (a.k.a. PBabatha)—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

obj. obj. = object, objective

Hs Hs = Similitudes

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Horst Horst = PvanderHorst, s. Ps.-Phocyl(ides)—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

  1. fr. = from

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

gener. gener. = generally

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Dio Chrys Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Olsson Olsson = BO., Papyrusbriefe aus d. frühen Römerzeit—List 4

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 96
